The original Hyatt Chalet · c. 1961
About the Inn

A Pasadena Original

Built in 1961 as a Hyatt Chalet — with its distinctive A-frame roof and diamond windows — La Casa Inn has welcomed guests to Pasadena for over six decades. Today it's a rare piece of mid-century roadside California: an independent inn on historic Route 66, minutes from Old Town, the Rose Bowl, and Caltech, offering comfortable modern rooms with genuine character you won't find at a chain.

Explore More

Experiences

Explore Our Neighborhood

Discover Pasadena's hidden gems and local wonders — from historic streets and lush gardens to iconic landmarks, all within minutes of La Casa Inn.

Old Town Pasadena
Historic Colorado Blvd with boutiques, restaurants, and weekend farmers markets.
5 min walk
Rose Bowl Stadium
Iconic venue for UCLA games, concerts, and the legendary monthly flea market.
10 min drive
Huntington Gardens
130 acres of botanical beauty and world-class art collections.
8 min drive
✿
Every January 1st

The World Comes
to Our Street.

Each New Year's Day, the Rose Parade rolls down Colorado Boulevard — the very street La Casa Inn calls home — with the route's grand finale just blocks from our door. Then all eyes turn to the Rose Bowl Game, minutes away. For one spectacular week, Pasadena becomes the center of the world, and we have the best seat in the house.

Rose Parade
January 1st  ·  8:00 AM  ·  Colorado Boulevard
Rose Bowl Game
January 1st  ·  Rose Bowl Stadium
When January 1 falls on a Sunday, both move to January 2 — a Pasadena tradition since 1893.

Rooms for Rose Parade week are our most requested of the year — and they go early.
Reservations for these dates are taken by phone only.

☎  Call (626) 449-1122 to Reserve
Take a Look Around

Gallery & History

La Casa Inn began its life in 1961 as a Hyatt Chalet — part of a short-lived and now much-loved chapter of American roadside hospitality. Its signature A-frame lobby, crowned with colorful diamond windows, was designed to catch the eye of travelers cruising Route 66 through Pasadena.

More than sixty years later, the A-frame still stands — one of the last of its kind in Southern California. The stained-glass panels still glow at night, welcoming guests just as they did when tailfins filled the parking lot.

Today, La Casa Inn remains proudly independent: a genuine piece of mid-century California, minutes from Old Town, the Rose Bowl, and Caltech — where every stay comes with a little history included.

Find Us

On Historic Route 66,
in the heart of Pasadena.

Set on East Colorado Boulevard — the original Route 66 — La Casa Inn puts you minutes from Old Town Pasadena, the Rose Bowl, Caltech, and Pasadena City College, with easy access to the 210 freeway and all of Los Angeles beyond.

2800 E Colorado Blvd
Pasadena, CA 91107
Open in Maps →
Lights. Camera. Pasadena.

The Backlot on Route 66

Some locations have to be dressed to look like the past. La Casa Inn simply is the past — a 1961 A-frame with stained-glass diamonds, an open-air courtyard, and a pool straight out of a Slim Aarons photograph. That's why location scouts keep finding their way to East Colorado Boulevard, and why our little corner of Pasadena has appeared in feature films, network dramas, streaming series, daytime television, and national commercials.

Production Spotlight

Poolman

For his directorial debut, Chris Pine turned our courtyard and pool into the sun-bleached Los Angeles of Darren Barrenman — writing, directing, and starring in scenes shot right here at the inn. For a few weeks, the A-frame traded overnight guests for camera cranes.

La Casa Inn appears in the film's official location credits — our name in lights, or at least on IMDb.

View the credit on IMDb →
Selected Credits
This Is UsNBC · Season 6The Emmy-winning family drama brought its final season to our doorstep.
PhysicalApple TV+Our mid-century bones were a natural fit for the '80s-set series.
The Vampire DiariesThe CWEven vampires need a place to check in.
ParenthoodNBCThe Bravermans' California is our California.
General HospitalABCDaytime television's longest-running drama, on location.
SubaruNational CommercialThe A-frame, coast to coast in 30 seconds.
King BachDigital ContentA new generation of storytellers, same iconic backdrop.
